title: docs(synergy-tracker): add synergy-bm-format reference doc
description: Create skills/synergy-tracker/references/synergy-bm-format.md with BM routing rules, section structure, entry transforms, and edit_note gotchas for synergy promotion. Mirrors upstream-tracker's basic-memory-friction-format.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- skills/synergy-tracker/references/synergy-bm-format.md exists
- Documents BM routing rules: which sibling entity-note types receive ## Cross-Project Synergy sections, fallback when bm-entity is absent in the registry
- Documents section structure (mirrors basic-memory-friction-format.md's structure: subsection scheme, ordering, placeholders)
- Documents entry transforms (SYNERGY entry → BM observation/relation rendering)
- Documents edit_note gotchas (append+section EOF bug; find_replace and insert_before_section preferred)
- npm run check passes (remark frail, validate-plugin.mjs, shellcheck/shfmt, check-hooks)
close_reason: Created skills/synergy-tracker/references/synergy-bm-format.md mirroring upstream-tracker's basic-memory-friction-format.md: target type routing (sibling project notes via bm-entity field with projects/ fallback), target section structure (## Cross-Project Synergy with four subsections + Resolved), generalization transforms (strip paths/dates/local-triage; keep Status/Convergence path/Readiness/Priority/Effort enums), and edit_note gotchas (append+section EOF bug, find_replace anchoring, expected_replacements=1, read_note before edit). npm run check passes.

title: feat(synergy-tracker): add promote-to-basic-memory workflow
description: Add workflow 5 that writes ## Cross-Project Synergy sections to sibling project entity notes in BM. Completes the BM integration planned since v0.9.0. The bm-entity registry field becomes functional.
acceptance_criteria:
- New workflow 5 (Promote to Basic Memory) documented in skills/synergy-tracker/SKILL.md
- Writes '## Cross-Project Synergy' sections to sibling project entity notes (parallel to upstream-tracker workflow 6's '## Upstream Friction' sections in package/tool notes)
- Honors the bm-entity registry field; degrades gracefully when bm-entity is absent or BM tools unavailable
- Workflow 5 is referenced in MEMORY.md / CLAUDE.md / README sections that currently say 'workflow 5 (Promote to BM) planned'
- Annotation semantics consistent with upstream-tracker: only this workflow's prune pass moves entries between sections
- npm run check passes; plugin-validator + skill-reviewer agents find no regressions

title: feat(synergy-tracker): add trend-review workflow
description: Add workflow 4 (trend review) to synergy-tracker for quarterly SYNERGY file analysis. Replaces the interim workaround of running W2 manually at trend-review boundaries.
acceptance_criteria:
- New workflow 4 (Trend Review) documented in skills/synergy-tracker/SKILL.md
- Cadence aligned with the every-4th-sprint trend-review convention used by retrospective and upstream-tracker
- Surfaces aggregate signals across all SYNERGY-*.md files: status drift, stale Last verified dates, ready extraction candidates that haven't moved
- Replaces the interim workaround of running workflow 2 (Review) manually at trend-review boundaries
- Output integrates with /retrospective trend-review sprint flow (cross-referenced in retrospective skill prose)
- npm run check passes; skill-reviewer finds no regressions
close_reason: Added Workflow 4 (Trend Review) to synergy-tracker SKILL.md mirroring upstream-tracker structure: input signals (glob, count, age), processing steps (drift audit, reciprocation check, status sweep, BM cross-reference, dormancy scaling), Trend Review entry format, and integration with /retrospective. Updated Sprint Workflow Integration to reflect that only workflow 5 remains planned.

title: validate-plugin: cross-reference allowed-tools against SKILL.md tool mentions
description:
Cross-check mcp__*__* patterns mentioned in skill/agent prose against the allowed-tools frontmatter. Pattern to follow: vp-claude's validate-plugin.mjs already implements this — port it. This gap has caused bugs in 3 consecutive sprints (v0.4.0 missing Write, v0.5.0 missing Write+Grep, v0.6.0 missing read_note).

## Acceptance Criteria

- validate-plugin.mjs scans SKILL.md and agent .md body text for mcp__*__* tool references
- Any MCP tool referenced in prose but missing from allowed-tools/tools fails validation
- npm run check catches the class of bug that required manual skill-reviewer in v0.4.0–v0.6.0
- No false positives on MCP tools mentioned in prose examples or documentation (only match tool call patterns)
close_reason: Ported auditToolReferences() from vp-claude — cross-checks mcp__*__* patterns in prose against allowed-tools/tools. Caught a real bug on first run (write_note in prose but not in allowlist).

title: vendor-sync: add Write and Grep to allowed-tools
description: Skill reviewer flagged missing tools. Write is needed for UPSTREAM file creation edge cases, Grep for changelog keyword matching in Step 7. Same class of bug as the upstream-tracker Write omission caught in v0.4.0.
close_reason: Fixed in v0.5.1

title: Audit PreCompact hook for write_note overwrite guard
description: Basic Memory write_note is no longer idempotent — requires overwrite=True for existing notes. Verify vp-beads PreCompact hook and retrospective skill handle this correctly. Silent failure risk.
close_reason: Converted PreCompact from broken prompt hook to command hook with precompact.sh; added overwrite guard to retro Step 7

title: add Write to upstream-tracker allowed-tools
description: upstream-tracker SKILL.md workflow 1 (Log a new entry) creates new UPSTREAM-*.md files for non-vendor packages that don't yet exist. Creating a new file requires the Write tool, but only Read/Edit/Glob/Grep/Bash are in allowed-tools. Edit cannot create files from scratch. This means the 'create it with this template' step silently fails when Write is not available.

title: bump plugin.json and CHANGELOG to v0.4.0
description: PreCompact hook was added and shipped (b604e21) but plugin.json still says 0.3.0 and CHANGELOG has no v0.4.0 entry. MEMORY.md already says 0.4.0. Needs: plugin.json version bump, CHANGELOG entry, marketplace.json bump in vp-claude (again).

title: feat(validate-plugin): convention check for naked workflow N references missing parenthetical name
description:
RETRO-10 lesson 1: convention enforcement is a structural gate-job, not an agent-job. Wave 1 + Wave 2 of Sprint 10 produced 5 HIGH naming-convention violations between them — all the same class ("workflow N" without "(Name)" parenthetical mandated by CLAUDE.md). Agents systematically miss this rule despite explicit prompts; reviewers always catch it. Surface earlier with an automated check.

## Acceptance Criteria

- scripts/validate-plugin.mjs adds a check that scans skill prose for "workflow N" patterns missing the immediately-following parenthetical "(Name)" form
- Pattern: \\bworkflow [0-9]+\\b not followed by " (" within the same paragraph
- Suggested allowlist for legitimate exceptions: workflow numbers in tables, workflow numbers in code blocks, workflow numbers cited inside other parentheticals
- Tests in scripts/check-hooks.mjs (or new scripts/check-conventions.mjs) cover the new check with 3+ fixture cases
- npm run check fails on naked references; the 5 Sprint 10 fix-agent corrections are exactly the cases this check would have caught
- README/CLAUDE.md note the new check under the validation section

title: decision: vendor-sync step 8b should consume external_ref to verify upstream closure before annotating BM
description:
## Decision

Extend vendor-sync step 8b (the auto-resolve loop that annotates BM `## Upstream Friction` entries) to look for `external_ref:` lines in BM friction entries. When present, verify upstream closure via `bd github pull <ref>` (or the appropriate per-tracker variant) before annotating. Use upstream closure as a stronger resolution signal than vendor diff alone.

## Rationale

The bd v1.0.0 Integration Charter establishes `external_ref` as the canonical first-class field for tracking external tracker IDs (`gh-NNN`, `jira-PROJ-NNN`, `linear-NNN`, `ado:NNNNN`). If a friction entry was filed upstream via the related upstream-tracker workflow 6 (Promote) decision (companion bead), it will carry an `external_ref`. vendor-sync should use this to make stronger resolution claims: "vendor diff resolved AND upstream issue closed" is much stronger than "vendor diff resolved" alone, and gives users confidence that the friction is truly behind them.

Source: vp-beads-0e9.2 spike findings (Charter implication #3).

## Alternatives Considered

- **Skip external_ref consumption entirely** — rejected. Without it, vendor-sync auto-resolves can flap (vendor reverts, friction returns) with no upstream signal to corroborate.
- **Use external_ref as the SOLE resolution signal** — rejected. Vendor diff is the actual ground truth for the sync; upstream closure is corroborating evidence, not a substitute.
- **Implement a separate "verify external_ref" workflow** — rejected. Belongs in the same auto-resolve pass to avoid splitting one decision across two skill invocations.

## Trigger to implement

Companion bead (upstream-tracker workflow 6 `bd <tracker> push` integration) ships first — without filings, no `external_ref` exists in BM entries. Until then, file but don't implement.
close_reason: captured decision; depends on companion 0e9.13 shipping per stated trigger

title: decision: upstream-tracker workflow 6 should optionally suggest bd github push after BM promote
description:
## Decision

In upstream-tracker workflow 6 (Promote to Basic Memory), add an optional decision branch: when a friction entry has matured into a filable upstream issue (e.g. has clear repro + concrete fix), suggest `bd github push` (or the appropriate per-tracker variant) to file it upstream after the BM promote completes. Document the `external_ref` round-trip so the BM `## Upstream Friction` entry can carry the filed issue's `gh-NNN` shorthand for later cross-referencing.

## Rationale

The bd v1.0.0 Integration Charter (docs/INTEGRATION_CHARTER.md) legitimizes `bd <tracker> push` as the canonical pipe for issue-shaped upstream contributions. vp-beads currently routes everything through `UPSTREAM-*.md` → BM `## Upstream Friction` sections, with no path to actually filing upstream. Adding the optional `bd github push` step (and the corresponding `external_ref` annotation in BM) closes the loop: friction discovered → tracked locally → promoted to BM → filed upstream → resolution syncs back via vendor-sync (see related bead for vendor-sync external_ref consumption).

Source: vp-beads-0e9.2 spike findings (Charter implication #1).

## Alternatives Considered

- **Make `bd <tracker> push` the default** — rejected. Many friction entries are observations or workarounds the user is not ready to file; default-on would create noise upstream.
- **File a separate dedicated workflow** — rejected. The decision point is already inside workflow 6 ("is this generalizable enough to promote?"); adding an adjacent "is this filable upstream?" check belongs in the same gate.
- **Skip the integration entirely** — rejected. Without it, vp-beads users have no plugin-supported path to upstream contribution; everything goes through manual `gh issue create`.

## Trigger to implement

A vp-beads user (or the maintainer) wants UPSTREAM friction to round-trip to a GitHub issue. Until then, file but don't implement.
close_reason: captured decision; implementation deferred until a vp-beads user wants UPSTREAM friction round-trip per stated trigger

title: decision: adopt bd swarm validate as DAG layer in swarm-wave when sprint scope is an epic
description:
## Decision

Inside swarm-wave workflow 1 step 4f, when the sprint scope is an epic, call `bd swarm validate <epic-id> --json` to obtain the dependency-DAG ready_fronts, then **intersect** those fronts with the file-contention map (workflow 4) to produce final wave assignments. Reject `bd swarm create/status/list` — the swarm-molecule artifact carries no file-scope or gate state and adds bookkeeping without value.

## Rationale

`bd swarm validate` is a Kahn topo-sort with level tracking that beads computes for free. swarm-wave's workflow 1 step 4f currently re-derives the same ordering by reading `bd blocked`. Delegating the DAG layer eliminates duplication, picks up bd's existing warnings (cycles/orphans/disconnected subgraphs/incorrect dep direction), and keeps swarm-wave focused on its unique value-add (file-disjoint partitioning, contention thresholds, two-reviewer gate, RAM-based concurrency limits, ephemeral SWARM-NN.md tracking) — none of which bd swarm provides.

Source: vp-beads-0e9.1 spike findings.

## Alternatives Considered

- **Replace swarm-wave with bd swarm primitives** — rejected. bd swarm has no file-contention model, no quality gate, no agent isolation, no RAM ceilings. Would lose the entire value-add of the skill.
- **Stay fully independent (no delegation)** — rejected. Re-deriving DAG ordering when bd already computes it is duplicate logic and misses bd's built-in warnings.
- **Use `bd swarm create` to materialize a swarm-molecule per sprint** — rejected. The molecule artifact carries no file-scope or gate state; SWARM-NN.md remains the right tracking artifact.

## Trigger to revisit

Watch beads CHANGELOG for v1.1+ — if `bd swarm` gains file-contention input or post-wave-gate hooks, re-evaluate full delegation.
close_reason: captured decision; implementation deferred until next epic-scoped swarm sprint per stated trigger (decision body has full Decision/Rationale/Alternatives)

title: Story: adopt spike/story/milestone vocabulary in vp-beads skills and docs
description:
## User Story

As a vp-beads user, I want the plugin to recognize and use the new beads issue types (spike, story, milestone) so that the docs match the tool's actual vocabulary and skills give correct guidance on which type to use.

## Background

Beads v1.0+ added three new core issue types per `bd types`: spike (timeboxed investigation), story (user-centric framing), milestone (structural marker, no work). Type-specific required sections enforced by `validation.on-create=error`:
- spike: `## Goal` + `## Findings`
- story: `## Acceptance Criteria`
- milestone: (none — pure marker)
- decision: `## Decision` + `## Rationale` + `## Alternatives Considered`
- epic: `## Success Criteria`
- task / feature / bug: `## Acceptance Criteria` (bug also needs `## Steps to Reproduce`)

vp-beads skills should generate compliant issues per type. Currently CLAUDE.md lists only 6 types.

## Acceptance Criteria

- CLAUDE.md mentions all 9 core types with one-line descriptions
- MEMORY.md has a "Core types" section
- backlog-groomer skill recognizes spike/story/milestone in triage workflows
- Vocabulary alignment: backlog-groomer's "investigate-topic" workflow renamed/aligned to spike type
- All skills that file issues use the correct type and required sections
- Cross-reference required-sections table from BM note `brew-beads`
close_reason: vocabulary adoption complete: skill (Agent 2A), CLAUDE.md table (Agent 2B), MEMORY.md core types (Agent 2B)

title: Story: add bd find-duplicates to backlog-groomer triage workflow
description:
## User Story

As a maintainer triaging the backlog, I want backlog-groomer to surface near-duplicate issues so I can merge or supersede them before they fragment the backlog.

## Background

`bd find-duplicates` (alias `find-dups`) uses two-stage similarity: mechanical Jaccard tokenization (free, default, threshold 0.5) and optional AI semantic comparison via Claude (`--method=ai`, requires `ai.api_key`). AI method uses mechanical pre-filtering to reduce calls.

Distinct from `bd duplicates` (exact content match only).

## Acceptance Criteria

- backlog-groomer's "review-and-triage" workflow includes a `bd find-duplicates` step
- Documentation explains the two methods (mechanical default, AI via `--method=ai` requires `ai.api_key`)
- Default threshold guidance (0.5 = balanced; 0.4 = more results)
- Output presented to user with merge/supersede recommendations using `bd supersede` or `bd duplicate`
close_reason: bd find-duplicates step inserted in workflow 1; threshold + AI cost caveat in heuristics

title: Spike: investigate bd prime double-fire in SessionStart
description:
## Goal

Identify the source of duplicate `bd prime` output observed in SessionStart reminders and recommend a fix.

## Findings

Three independent hook configurations on this machine register SessionStart commands; **two of them run `bd prime`** identically:

1. `/Users/pelle/Sites/ai/vp-beads/hooks/hooks.json` lines 40-51 — runs `session-start.sh`, **does NOT** call `bd prime` (sensitive-file warnings + dormancy nudges + trend-review reminders only). vp-beads is innocent.
2. `/Users/pelle/.claude/plugins/cache/beads-marketplace/beads/1.0.3/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` lines 19-28 — runs `bd prime` on SessionStart; lines 30-40 also runs `bd prime` on PreCompact.
3. `/Users/pelle/.claude/settings.json` lines 97-107 — runs `bd prime` on SessionStart (installed via `bd setup claude --global`, predates the marketplace plugin).

**Root cause:** SessionStart fires `bd prime` twice — once from upstream `beads` plugin (config 2), once from global `~/.claude/settings.json` (config 3). The settings.json registration is redundant since the plugin now ships the same hook. Cost: ~1.5–3k tokens wasted per session. Visible at top of any session as the duplicate `# Beads Workflow Context` block.

Per Claude Code hook docs, multiple hooks for the same event all fire and their `additionalContext` outputs are concatenated — so two SessionStart hooks running `bd prime` produce two identical context blocks.

PreCompact fires `bd prime` only once (only config 2 registers PreCompact).

**Fix:** remove the SessionStart `bd prime` hook block from `~/.claude/settings.json`. Surgical edit, preserve all other settings. Verify by counting `# Beads Workflow Context` blocks in next session bootstrap (should be 1, not 2).

**No upstream beads bug** — the marketplace plugin's hook is correct. The issue is a stale global config left over from `bd setup claude --global`. (Optional FR upstream: `bd setup claude --check` could detect when the global hook duplicates the now-installed marketplace plugin's hook and offer to remove it. Dropped for now per YAGNI; revive if a second user reports the same.)

**Cross-reference:** update BM note `engineering/agents/beads-marketplace-beads-plugin` — replace the unconfirmed-source [gotcha] with the resolved root cause.

## Background

In sessions in this project, `# Beads Workflow Context` (the `bd prime` output) appears twice in SessionStart reminders. vp-beads's session-start.sh does NOT call bd prime (verified by reading hooks/session-start.sh). The upstream `beads` Claude Code plugin registers a single SessionStart hook running `bd prime`. So one of: (a) the upstream beads plugin is registered twice, (b) Claude Code SessionStart:clear has an auto-trigger for `.beads/` that fires bd prime, (c) shadow installation, (d) something else.

If double-fire occurs, ~1.5–3k tokens of context are wasted per session. Captured as a [gotcha] in BM note `beads-marketplace: beads plugin`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Source of duplicate identified
- If vp-beads bug: fix proposed and filed as separate issue
- If upstream beads or Claude Code: logged via /upstream-tracker
- BM observation in `beads-marketplace: beads plugin` updated with the resolved cause
close_reason: user applied manual ~/.claude/settings.json edit removing redundant SessionStart bd prime block. Full verification (count of '# Beads Workflow Context' blocks = 1, not 2) requires a fresh session — visual confirmation deferred to next session bootstrap

title: Spike: bd v1.0.0 Integration Charter — read and digest
description:
## Goal

Read the Integration Charter (added in beads v1.0.0, commit `5d524cf7` "Add Integration Charter: define scope boundary for tracker integrations") and identify implications for vp-beads's upstream-tracker, synergy-tracker, and any other workflow that touches external trackers.

## Findings

Charter source: `gastownhall/beads@5d524cf7:docs/INTEGRATION_CHARTER.md` (88 lines, added 2026-03-24, shipped in v1.0.0 on 2026-04-03; upstream bead `bd-9go`).

**Core claim:** tracker integrations (GitHub, GitLab, Jira, Linear, Azure DevOps, Notion) are an **adoption bridge, not a product**. They lower the barrier for teams already using these trackers, not replace those trackers' UIs or workflows. Differentiator bd protects: dependency-aware issue tracking for AI agents.

**In-scope:** (a) bidirectional polled metadata sync (title/status/assignee/priority/labels + dependency relationships) with three deterministic conflict-resolution strategies (`ConflictTimestamp`/`ConflictLocal`/`ConflictExternal` → `--prefer-newer`/`--prefer-github`/`--prefer-local`); (b) one-way bulk import for adoption/migration; (c) reliable error handling (exponential backoff with jitter, response-size limits, context cancellation, pagination guards, terminal sanitization). Plus six design guidelines for new integrations and a Decision Log retro-justifying four foundational calls dated 2026-03-24.

**Out-of-scope:** webhook gateways or real-time event systems (polled minutes-to-hours sync sufficient); cross-tracker orchestration (redirected to Zapier/n8n/GitHub Actions); attachment/binary content sync ("CDN problem"); full comment or thread mirroring (causes duplicate notifications, attribution problems); credential vaults; UI parity features (dashboards, burndown charts, sprint boards — "the CLI and JSON output are the interface").

External tracker IDs first-class via `external_ref` field with shorthand parsing: `gh-123`, `jira-PROJ-456`, `linear-789`, `ado:NNNNN`. Two distinct integration paths in the bd binary: `bd github` uses GitHub REST API directly with `github.token`; `bd gate check --type=gh:*` shells out to `gh` CLI.

## Implications for vp-beads

Each filed as a separate follow-up bead under epic vp-beads-0e9:

1. **upstream-tracker workflow 6 (Promote)** — add optional branch suggesting `bd github push` after BM promote when entry has matured into a filable upstream issue; document `external_ref` round-trip so BM entries can carry `gh-NNN` shorthand. (P2, decision)
2. **synergy-tracker SKILL.md** — cite Charter as design rationale for cross-project tracking living in BM, not bd ("no cross-tracker orchestration"). (P3, doc-only task)
3. **vendor-sync step 8b** — consume `external_ref` when present; verify upstream closure via `bd github pull gh-NNN` before annotating. (P2, decision)
4. **swarm-wave workflow 1 (Plan)** — note that GitHub-mirrored beads sync via `bd github sync` post-wave, not via swarm-wave itself. (P3, scope-guard task)
5. **MEMORY.md + brew-beads BM note** — capture Charter as recurring reference. (P3, task)

Also: append two `[architecture]` observations to `brew/brew-beads` `### Tracker Integrations` block (in-scope/out-of-scope split + `external_ref` shorthand). Add `relates_to [[brew-beads]]` to `engineering/agents/vp-plugins-vp-beads-and-vp-knowledge`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Charter summary captured (1-2 paragraphs)
- 3-5 concrete implications for vp-beads identified (each with affected skill and proposed action)
- Generalizable patterns captured in Basic Memory under `engineering/agents/` if applicable
close_reason: Spike findings populated; 1zu fix applied. 0e9.3 stays open pending manual settings.json edit by user.

title: Spike: bd swarm primitives vs vp-beads swarm-wave skill
description:
## Goal

Determine whether vp-beads's swarm-wave skill should delegate wave planning to native `bd swarm validate`, replace its workflow with bd swarm primitives, or stay independent — and why.

## Findings

`bd swarm validate --json` (added in beads v0.41.0) is a DAG-only planner: Kahn's topological sort with level tracking, scoped to a single epic, outputs `ready_fronts[]`, `max_parallelism`, `estimated_sessions`, warnings on cycles/orphans/disconnected subgraphs. It has **no file-contention model** — bd would happily place two issues touching `src/foo.ts` in the same wave.

vp-beads's `swarm-wave` skill (5 workflows, 6 reference docs) uniquely owns: file-disjoint partitioning (workflow 4 — file-contention map with LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH thresholds, section-level analysis for >500-line files), two-reviewer post-wave gate (workflow 3 — code-reviewer + domain-specialized reviewer, confidence thresholds 60/80), agent concurrency limits with RAM-based ceilings + 4-phase backpressure, ephemeral SWARM-NN.md tracking, research-wave with dedup. Tightly coupled to repo state (working tree, package.json, source code) — cannot run on synthetic DAG descriptions alone.

Overlap: only swarm-wave workflow 1 step 4f ("issues that block dependents go in earlier waves") — pure DAG topo-sort, exactly what `bd swarm validate` already computes for free.

`bd swarm create/status/list` produce a "swarm molecule" issue (`MolTypeSwarm` linked to the epic via `relates-to`) but the artifact carries no file-scope or gate state. Not useful as a substitute for SWARM-NN.md.

## Decision

**Stay independent, with one delegation.** Adopt `bd swarm validate --json` inside swarm-wave workflow 1 step 4f as the dependency-DAG layer **when sprint scope is an epic** — intersect its `ready_fronts` with the file-contention map and split/merge accordingly. Reject `bd swarm create/status/list`. Trigger to revisit: bd swarm gains file-contention input or post-wave-gate hooks (watch CHANGELOG for v1.1+).

Follow-up filed as separate `decision`-typed bead.

## Background

Beads v1.0+ ships native swarm primitives: `bd swarm create/list/status/validate`. `bd swarm validate` reports ready fronts (waves of parallel work), estimated worker-sessions, max parallelism, and warnings on cycles/orphans/disconnected subgraphs/incorrect dependency direction. This is essentially the wave-planning algorithm built into beads.

vp-beads's swarm-wave skill currently hand-rolls wave planning with file-contention analysis (the unique value-add: file-disjoint partitioning, contention thresholds, two-reviewer post-wave gate). There's clear functional overlap on the planning side.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Functional overlap mapped (what bd swarm covers vs what swarm-wave uniquely covers)
- Decision: (a) delegate planning to `bd swarm validate`, (b) replace swarm-wave with bd swarm primitives, (c) stay independent — with rationale
- File a follow-up `decision`-typed issue capturing the chosen direction
close_reason: Spike findings populated; 1zu fix applied. 0e9.3 stays open pending manual settings.json edit by user.

title: Epic: Adopt beads v1.0+ capabilities in vp-beads
description:
v1.0.0 of beads (released 2026-04-03) added significant new surface area that vp-beads should evaluate and selectively adopt. This epic groups investigation spikes and adoption stories.

Source: cross-session research captured in Basic Memory note `brew-beads` and `beads-marketplace: beads plugin`. Focused dives on: issue type vocabulary expansion (spike/story/milestone), native swarm primitives (`bd swarm`), async coordination gates (`bd gate`), semantic deduplication (`bd find-duplicates`), two-tier persistence (wisps + `bd promote`), GitHub/tracker integrations, the Integration Charter, and the Claude rules engine.

Children explore impact areas individually; this epic stays open until all child issues are closed or deferred with rationale.

## Success Criteria

- All child spikes resolved (decision recorded or rationale for deferral)
- All child stories either delivered or deliberately dropped with reason
- Documentation (CLAUDE.md, MEMORY.md, README.md) reflects adopted capabilities
- Basic Memory notes (brew-beads, beads-marketplace: beads plugin) updated with outcomes

title: feat(skills): new /sibling-sync skill for bilateral SYNERGY/UPSTREAM reconciliation
acceptance_criteria: New /sibling-sync skill ships with at minimum capabilities 1-3 (diff entries, flag status conflicts, surface gaps); workflow 5 in synergy-tracker (batch reciprocation) defers to /sibling-sync if shipped; degrades gracefully when sibling repos not accessible; check-hooks/remark pass; plugin-validator + skill-reviewer agents find no regressions.
close_reason: new /sibling-sync skill created with 4 workflows: discover, sync SYNERGY, sync UPSTREAM, apply reciprocation batch (opt-in). Read-only by default with --auto-reciprocate opt-in path. Cross-skill boundaries documented. CLAUDE.md updated with skill summary and sprint workflow cycle integration.

title: feat(synergy-tracker,vendor-sync): extend registry schema with local-path field and .local.json companion
description:
The current synergy-registry.json schema (name, file, remote, bm-entity, relationship) has no on-disk-location field; workflow 3 (Compare with sibling) hardcodes ../<sibling>/ as the path. Different machines have different layouts (~/Sites/<name>, ~/code/<name>, CI checkout paths, monorepo subdirectories).

Two complementary additions needed:

(1) Add OPTIONAL local-path field to each entry — sensible default for the common ../<name> layout, recorded explicitly so the skill doesn't have to guess.

(2) Support a gitignored .claude/synergy-registry.local.json companion file (object keyed by entry name) that overrides any field in the main registry — for users with non-standard local layouts. Mirrors the settings.local.json convention. Skill resolves: load synergy-registry.json, merge synergy-registry.local.json if present, then use merged result.

Same schema extension applies to vendor-registry.json for symmetry — vendor-sync currently also assumes a standard layout.

Without this, every workflow that touches sibling files (compare, the proposed /sibling-sync below, stale-row detection above) must re-prompt for paths or fail on non-standard layouts.

Source: ../vp-claude/UPSTREAM-vp-beads.md (entry 4, 2026-05-04). Blocks the proposed /sibling-sync skill (separate issue).
acceptance_criteria: synergy-registry.json schema documents optional local-path; .local.json companion documented; reference docs in skills/synergy-tracker/references/synergy-entry-format.md updated; same applied to vendor-registry; existing registries continue to work without local-path; check-hooks/remark pass.
notes: Implementation note (added 2026-05-04): the vp-beads .gitignore already contains '.claude/*.local.*' which covers any .local.json companion file. When this work lands, verify that pattern exists (or document the requirement) and ensure the skill prose tells users to add such an entry when introducing .local.json companions in their own projects. The .local.json companion file MUST never be committed since it encodes machine-specific paths.
close_reason: registry schema extended with optional local-path field and .local.json companion override pattern; backward compatible; documented in both CLAUDE.md convention sections; symmetric across synergy-registry and vendor-registry

title: fix(retrospective): add 'npm run check' to test-command waterfall
description:
Three-sprint repeat (RETRO-17, RETRO-18, RETRO-19 in vp-claude) of 'npm test' failing in projects whose health gate is 'npm run check' instead of 'npm test'.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. In a project whose package.json has a 'check' script but no 'test', 'test:node', or other variant (e.g., vp-knowledge, vp-beads, vp-git).
2. Run /retrospective.
3. The skill walks through the test waterfall in skills/retrospective/SKILL.md lines 84-88: 'npm test' → 'npm run test:node' → 'npm run test'.
4. None of the three commands match the project's actual health gate.
5. The retrospective records the failure, but no fix-path exists in the skill, so the same failure recurs next sprint.

## Expected

Waterfall should also try 'npm run check' as a fourth fallback so validation-suite projects (vp-knowledge, vp-beads, vp-git) succeed.

## Concrete change

skills/retrospective/SKILL.md lines 84-88 — append a fourth fallback 'npm run check'. Retrospective is the right place to fix this because retros surface the failure but the failure has no other fix-path — without this it repeats every sprint.

## Source

../vp-claude/UPSTREAM-vp-beads.md (entry 6, 2026-05-04). vp-claude already tracks a local fix as vp-claude-9mr P3.
acceptance_criteria: Waterfall in skills/retrospective/SKILL.md includes 'npm run check' as the fourth fallback; remark passes; the fix lands in time for the next vp-beads retrospective so this project also benefits.
close_reason: Spike findings populated; 1zu fix applied. 0e9.3 stays open pending manual settings.json edit by user.

title: feat(synergy-tracker): add domain-fit test to 'They Have / We Don't' section guidance
description:
Section description currently reads 'capabilities in the sibling project that this project lacks and may want to adopt' — too permissive. It admits category errors where the sibling has a capability in a different domain (vp-beads's swarm-wave for sprint orchestration; vp-beads's vendor-sync for vendored content) that don't fit because the comparing project has neither the underlying need nor the implementation. vp-claude's Sprint 19 produced 2 such false-positive candidates that the user had to dismiss.

Concrete change: add a one-sentence test to the section guidance — 'Pass test: this project has the underlying need but lacks the implementation. Fail: the sibling has a capability in a different domain than this project's.' Apply in SKILL.md and references/synergy-entry-format.md.

Source: ../vp-claude/UPSTREAM-vp-beads.md (entry 2, 2026-05-04).
acceptance_criteria: Section description in SKILL.md and synergy-entry-format.md includes the pass/fail test; remark passes; example entries don't violate the test.
close_reason: Added domain-fit pass/fail test to 'They Have / We Don't' section guidance in both SKILL.md and references/synergy-entry-format.md. Cited Sprint 19 worked failures (swarm-wave, vendor-sync) as evidence of category errors the test catches.

title: feat(synergy-tracker): mandate bilateral reciprocation in workflow 1 (Log) prose
description:
Workflow 1 (Log) tells the user to write entries from this project's POV but does not require that, when the sibling has already written entries on their side, those entries be reciprocated here. Asymmetric tracking silently misses drift.

vp-claude's Sprint 19 reciprocation pass on their SYNERGY-vp-beads.md re-verified our 10 shared-pattern entries from their side and surfaced 3 actively drifting artifacts (validate-plugin.mjs 358 vs 333 lines; check-hooks.mjs 366 vs 284 lines; npm-run-all2 row needing refresh) and 1 stale aligned row that vp-beads had no way to detect (PreCompact retired in vp-knowledge v0.28.0). Our local SYNERGY-vp-knowledge.md was just refreshed (2026-05-04) on the back of those findings.

Concrete change: append to workflow 1 (Log) — 'When the sibling has written entries from their side, reciprocate by re-verifying each from this project's angle and recording your verification dates and any drift you observe. Do not skip duplicates — the reciprocation IS the verification step.'

Source: ../vp-claude/UPSTREAM-vp-beads.md (entry 1, 2026-05-04).
acceptance_criteria: Workflow 1 step list includes a reciprocation mandate paragraph; remark/lint passes; vocabulary stays consistent (workflow N (name) format); skill-reviewer finds no regressions.
close_reason: Added bilateral reciprocation mandate paragraph to workflow 1 (Log) after the Structured fields subsection. Includes verbatim BM quote and Sprint 19 evidence (3 actively drifting artifacts plus 1 stale aligned row surfaced by reciprocation pass). Mandates prompting user to file reciprocal entry on sibling project.

title: chore: add MIT LICENSE file
description: plugin.json declares license: MIT but no LICENSE file exists at project root. Standard open-source hygiene.
close_reason: MIT LICENSE file created

title: refactor(hooks): convert PostToolUseFailure prompt to command hook

title: New skill: synergy-tracker for cross-project collaboration tracking
description: Create skills/synergy-tracker/ with SKILL.md (W1 log, W2 review, W3 compare) and references/synergy-entry-format.md. Manages SYNERGY-*.md files tracking shared patterns, divergences, extraction candidates, and capability gaps between sibling projects. Permanent files, .claude/synergy-registry.json discovery. BM integration (W4 trend review, W5 promote) deferred to v0.10.0.
close_reason: Created skills/synergy-tracker/SKILL.md (W1-W3) and references/synergy-entry-format.md

title: Fix early-exit bug in session-start.sh suppressing dormancy nudge
description: The count -eq 0 guard at line 10-12 exits the entire script when no RETRO files exist. This suppresses the dormancy nudge in new repos that have UPSTREAM files but no retrospectives yet. Restructure so dormancy nudge fires regardless of retro count.
close_reason: session-start.sh rewritten with sensitive-file warning, early-exit fix, and SYNERGY dormancy nudge

title: session-start: warn if sensitive beads files are git-tracked
description: hooks/session-start.sh should check if .beads/interactions.jsonl or .beads/.beads-credential-key are tracked by git, and emit a systemMessage warning with remediation commands. Must fire before the retro-count early exit to work in all repos.
close_reason: session-start.sh rewritten with sensitive-file warning, early-exit fix, and SYNERGY dormancy nudge

title: Add PostToolUseFailure hook for BM error recovery
description:
Now that upstream-tracker, vendor-sync, and sprint-review all call BM MCP tools, need structured error classification when BM is unavailable or writes fail. Pattern: vp-claude's 5-category classification (server unavailable, invalid argument, note not found, permission error, unknown). New files: hooks/post-bm-failure.sh + hooks.json update.

## Acceptance Criteria

- PostToolUseFailure hook in hooks.json matching mcp__basic-memory__write_note|edit_note|read_note|search_notes
- Shell script classifies errors into categories and emits systemMessage with recovery guidance
- npm run check passes (shellcheck + shfmt on the new script)
- Hook timeout is reasonable (10s max)
close_reason: Added PostToolUseFailure prompt hook matching all 4 BM MCP tools. Classifies errors into 5 categories with vp-beads-specific recovery guidance (references W1/W3/W6/W7 and vendor-sync 8b).

title: vendor-sync: replace git diff HEAD~1 with pre-pull hash
description: Step 7 changelog cross-reference uses HEAD~1 which assumes the subtree pull created exactly one commit. If Step 4 conflict resolution adds extra commits, this misses changelog changes. Capture pre-pull HEAD hash before Step 3 and diff against that.
close_reason: Fixed in v0.5.1

title: upstream-tracker: align severity vocabulary
description: Bracket notation uses blocking/degraded/minor (line 119) but structured Severity field uses blocking/annoying/cosmetic (line 140). These describe the same concept with different terms. Align to one vocabulary — recommend keeping blocking/degraded/minor as the established terms and updating the structured field table to match.
close_reason: Fixed in v0.5.1

title: Add changelog-aware auto-resolution to vendor-sync
description: After git subtree pull, parse the CHANGELOG.md diff from upstream, AI-match against open UPSTREAM entries, and auto-resolve with confidence scoring. Pattern borrowed from DepsHub/Adaptly.
close_reason: Added Step 7 changelog cross-reference with confidence levels

title: document gitignored RETRO anchor edge case in retrospective skill
description: retrospective/SKILL.md Step 2 bash comment says 'correct for Sprint 1' when no prior RETRO file exists. It should also note the gitignore edge case: if RETRO-*.md files are gitignored, git log -1 --format=%H -- RETRO-*.md returns empty and the range silently expands to full history. The skill handles this gracefully but the comment is misleading. Fix: extend the comment to document both cases (no retros yet, and retros gitignored).
close_reason: Documented both edge cases in retrospective Step 2 comment

title: docs(swarm-wave): codify single-owner-per-issue + fix-loop budget patterns from RETRO-10
description:
Two RETRO-10 lessons need codification in the swarm-wave skill:

1. **Lesson 2 — Single-owner-per-issue beats wave-level file-disjoint partitioning.** When an issue's file scope spans what would otherwise be split across two agents (e.g. Sprint 10's vp-beads-0e9.6 spanning skill code + CLAUDE.md + MEMORY.md), assign the whole issue to one agent. Issue narrative coherence > wave-level file balance. Update skills/swarm-wave/SKILL.md workflow 1 step 4 (clustering rules) and references/file-contention-and-clustering.md.

2. **Lesson 5 — Autonomous wave execution should declare a fix-loop budget.** Workflow 3 step 6 currently says "After the fix agent completes, re-gate from step 1." Add an iteration cap: "If fix iterations exceed 2, halt and escalate to user instead of launching a third fix agent." Sprint 10 had 1 fix iteration per wave (within budget); the budget is preventive for future runaway gate failures.

## Acceptance Criteria

- skills/swarm-wave/SKILL.md workflow 1 step 4 has a sentence about single-owner-per-issue when an issue's scope crosses multiple files
- skills/swarm-wave/references/file-contention-and-clustering.md has a "Single owner per issue" subsection describing the trade-off
- skills/swarm-wave/SKILL.md workflow 3 step 6 has a fix-loop iteration cap (suggested: 2 iterations max)
- npm run check passes
- skill-reviewer agent finds no regressions

title: feat(hooks): surface open Dependabot alerts in SessionStart
description:
Dependabot alerts on the GitHub repo are currently invisible until git push prints them in remote output. A vp-beads SessionStart hook enhancement could query GitHub via gh CLI and include open alert counts in the additionalContext, surfacing them at session bootstrap rather than at push time.

Scope: optional, gated on gh CLI being available + repo having Dependabot configured. Should not fire if gh is missing or repo has no security alerts surface.

## Acceptance Criteria

- hooks/session-start.sh queries gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/dependabot/alerts (or equivalent) when gh is available
- If alerts exist: emit a single line summary (e.g. "[security] 1 moderate Dependabot alert") in additionalContext
- Skips silently if: gh missing, no GitHub remote, no alerts, or rate-limited
- Existing single-JSON output contract preserved (one object on stdout)
- check-hooks.mjs test covers the new path with mocked gh output

## Source

RETRO-9 "What could improve" — first signal for the moderate vulnerability surfaced this sprint came from git push remote output, not from Claude Code session context.

title: docs: capture bd Integration Charter as recurring reference in MEMORY.md and brew-beads BM note
description:
Two doc updates:

1. Add a one-line entry to ~/.claude/projects/-Users-pelle-Sites-ai-vp-beads/memory/MEMORY.md noting the bd Integration Charter (gastownhall/beads@5d524cf7:docs/INTEGRATION_CHARTER.md, shipped v1.0.0) so future sessions don't re-spike: "tracker sync is metadata-only, polled, no webhooks/comments/attachments — vp-beads owns the workflow-automation layer above bd."

2. Append two `[architecture]` observations to BM note `brew/brew-beads` `### Tracker Integrations` block: (a) in-scope/out-of-scope split from the Charter; (b) `external_ref` shorthand parsing across all 5 trackers (`gh-NNN`, `jira-PROJ-NNN`, `linear-NNN`, `ado:NNNNN`, `gitlab-NNN`).

Source: vp-beads-0e9.2 spike findings (Charter implication #5 + cross-references).

## Acceptance Criteria

- MEMORY.md contains the Charter reference line
- BM `brew/brew-beads` note has the two new `[architecture]` observations
- Optional: add `relates_to [[brew-beads]]` to BM note `engineering/agents/vp-plugins-vp-beads-and-vp-knowledge`
close_reason: Charter ref added to MEMORY.md + 2 [architecture] observations on brew-beads ### Tracker Integrations

title: docs(swarm-wave): note that GitHub-mirrored beads sync via bd github sync post-wave, not via swarm-wave
description:
Add a sentence to skills/swarm-wave/SKILL.md workflow 1 (Plan) noting that GitHub-mirrored beads sync via `bd github sync` after the wave completes — not via swarm-wave itself. This is a scope guard preventing future "can swarm-wave dispatch issues to GitHub?" requests from leaking GitHub-specific behavior into swarm-wave.

The bd v1.0.0 Integration Charter explicitly punts cross-tracker orchestration out of bd's scope; swarm-wave should follow the same boundary.

Source: vp-beads-0e9.2 spike findings (Charter implication #4).

## Acceptance Criteria

- skills/swarm-wave/SKILL.md workflow 1 has a single sentence noting `bd github sync` runs post-wave, not as part of swarm-wave
- Citation to the Integration Charter (or to the synergy-tracker note that cites it, once that lands)
- npm run check passes (remark frail)
close_reason: Added scope-guard note to swarm-wave SKILL.md workflow 1 (Plan a swarm sprint), placed as a Note immediately after step 7 and before workflow 2. Cites bd v1.0.0 Integration Charter (gastownhall/beads@5d524cf7:docs/INTEGRATION_CHARTER.md) as design rationale: cross-tracker orchestration (bd github sync) runs post-wave/post-sprint, not via swarm-wave. npm run check passes (17/17 tests, plugin/md/sh/hooks all green).

title: docs(synergy-tracker): cite bd Integration Charter as design rationale for cross-project tracking living in BM
description:
Add a one-line note to skills/synergy-tracker/SKILL.md citing the bd v1.0.0 Integration Charter (docs/INTEGRATION_CHARTER.md, gastownhall/beads@5d524cf7) as the design rationale for why cross-project tracking lives in BM rather than in bd: the Charter's "no cross-tracker orchestration" rule means bd will never grow a feature that routes a synergy item from project A's bd to project B's bd. synergy-tracker's `SYNERGY-*.md` + `## Cross-Project Synergy` BM section is exactly the workflow-automation layer the Charter punts to external tools.

Source: vp-beads-0e9.2 spike findings (Charter implication #2).

## Acceptance Criteria

- skills/synergy-tracker/SKILL.md has a single citation line referencing the Charter and its "no cross-tracker orchestration" stance as design rationale
- The citation appears in a context that explains why synergy-tracker exists (likely the skill's intro or "Relationship to..." section)
- npm run check passes (remark frail)
close_reason: Added single-paragraph Charter citation to Guidelines section explaining bd v1.0.0 Integration Charter no-cross-tracker-orchestration rule as design rationale for synergy-tracker living in BM rather than bd.

title: Task: verify bd init generates canonical gitignore in fresh dir, resolve FR if confirmed
description:
## Background

UPSTREAM-brew--beads.md has an open feature request from 2026-03-28: `bd init should auto-gitignore interactions.jsonl and .beads-credential-key`. As of bd v1.0.3, the canonical gitignore template that `bd doctor --fix --yes` writes DOES include both files (verified during sprint 8 infrastructure refresh). This suggests the FR may already be resolved for fresh installs — but `bd doctor --fix` is a recovery path; it doesn't prove that fresh `bd init` produces the same template.

This task verifies the resolution empirically.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Run `bd init` in a fresh scratch directory (e.g. `mktemp -d`)
- Read the generated `.beads/.gitignore`
- Confirm whether it includes `interactions.jsonl` AND `.beads-credential-key`
- If both present: delete the FR from UPSTREAM-brew--beads.md (per workflow 3 of upstream-tracker — "When an issue has been fixed upstream, simply delete it"); mention resolution in commit message
- If either missing: document the discrepancy as a comment on the existing FR; the FR remains open
- Time-box: 15 minutes

## Notes

- Surfaced in RETRO-8.md trend-review section as action item #1
- Lowest-effort UPSTREAM cleanup available

title: Task: triage 4 stale P4 issues from pre-vp-beads-0e9 backlog
description:
## Background

Sprint 8 trend review (RETRO-8.md) flagged 4 stale P4 issues, all 50+ days old, all from before the vp-beads-0e9 epic was filed. They've been rotting in the backlog without triage. Each needs one of: defer (still relevant, not urgent), close (obsolete or superseded), or promote (worth doing now, raise priority).

## Stale issues

- `vp-beads-emm` — add reference files for UPSTREAM and vendor-registry formats
- `vp-beads-3z0` — add PostToolUse hook for UPSTREAM-*.md write validation
- `vp-beads-dca` — add schemas/ directory for RETRO and UPSTREAM structures
- `vp-beads-xux` — observation normalization for UPSTREAM entries

## Acceptance Criteria

- Each of the 4 issues reviewed and assigned an outcome: defer with new target date, close with reason, OR promote with new priority
- For close: use `bd close <id> --reason="..."` so the rationale survives
- For defer: use `bd defer <id> --until="<date>"` to set a revival date
- For promote: `bd update <id> --priority=N` and `bd update <id> --notes="promoted from stale because..."`
- Update the retro after triage with the outcomes (1-2 sentences)
- Time-box: 30 minutes total

## Notes

- Surfaced in RETRO-8.md trend-review section as action item #2
- Standalone task (not under vp-beads-0e9 epic) — pre-existing backlog hygiene

title: Story: add bd doctor/lint/stale/orphans to retrospective trend-review workflow
description:
## User Story

As a maintainer running a trend-review sprint (every 4th), I want bd's full health vocabulary (infrastructure, template quality, lifecycle hygiene, broken refs) checked automatically so I catch drift across all dimensions before it propagates.

## Background

bd ships five distinct health checks, each with a focused scope:

| Command | Scope | What it catches |
|---------|-------|-----------------|
| `bd doctor` | Infrastructure | Outdated hooks, gitignore drift, remote config, Claude integration |
| `bd lint` | Template compliance | Issues missing required sections (`## Acceptance Criteria`, `## Steps to Reproduce`, etc.) per type |
| `bd stale` | Lifecycle | Issues with no recent activity (likely abandoned or forgotten) |
| `bd orphans` | Reference integrity | Issues referenced in commits but still open (typically completed-but-not-closed) |
| `bd graph check` | Dependency graph | Cycles, dangling refs, structural integrity (covered by sibling story vp-beads-0e9.8) |

The retrospective skill's trend-review workflow already runs a partial health audit ("bd stats, stale issues, blocked issues"). This story expands that to the full doctor/lint/stale/orphans set, with graph integrity covered by sibling story .8.

Drift example from 2026-05-04: a single `bd doctor --fix --yes` resolved 3 warnings — 5 hook files bumped v0.60.0 → v1.0.3, `.beads/.gitignore` reconciled to canonical patterns, project root `.gitignore` updated. Equivalent drift exists across the other dimensions (lint compliance shifts when validation rules tighten, stale issues accumulate when sprints skip backlog grooming, orphans appear when commits reference issues that aren't closed atomically).

## Acceptance Criteria

- retrospective skill's trend-review workflow runs all four checks (read-only first):
  - `bd doctor --json` — infrastructure
  - `bd lint --json` — template compliance per issue
  - `bd stale --json` — issues without recent activity
  - `bd orphans --json` — broken refs (verify the `--json` flag exists; fall back to text parsing if not)
- Each check's findings are summarized in the RETRO file under a unified "Health Audit" section (counts + top items per check)
- For checks with auto-fixes (currently `bd doctor --fix --yes`): prompts user before applying; never auto-applies
- For checks without auto-fixes (lint/stale/orphans require human triage): list affected issues with suggested actions (lint → update missing sections; stale → defer/close/work; orphans → close or unmark)
- Coordinates with sibling story vp-beads-0e9.8 (`bd graph check`) — both belong in the same Health Audit section; implement together to avoid duplicate structure
- Conditional on trend-review sprint detection (current sprint % 4 == 0)
- Documents the false-positive caveat for `bd doctor`'s Claude Hook Completeness check (see UPSTREAM-brew--beads.md)

## Related

- vp-beads-0e9.8 — sibling story adding `bd graph check` (dependency integrity); both belong in the same Health Audit section
- UPSTREAM-brew--beads.md — Claude Hook Completeness false-positive context

title: Story: add bd graph check to retrospective trend-review workflow
description:
## User Story

As a maintainer running a trend-review sprint (every 4th), I want graph integrity validated automatically so I catch dependency corruption before it propagates across sprints.

## Background

`bd graph check` is a CI-friendly integrity check (separate command from `bd graph` visualization). Returns exit 0 (clean) / 1 (issues). JSON output is schema-versioned for stable consumption: `{"clean": true, "cycles": null, "schema_version": 1, "summary": {"cycle_count": 0}}`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- retrospective skill's trend-review workflow runs `bd graph check --json`
- JSON output is parsed (schema_version: 1) and surfaced in the RETRO file
- Failures (cycles, orphans, dangling refs) generate fix recommendations
- Integration is conditional on trend-review sprint detection (current sprint number % 4 == 0)

title: Story: integrate decision type with retrospective decision-capture flow
description:
## User Story

As a maintainer running a retrospective, I want decisions surfaced during the sprint to be created as `decision`-typed beads (using the structured ADR template) rather than generic tasks — so they're queryable as ADRs and follow the supersede workflow.

## Background

`decision` is a core beads type. Required sections enforced: `## Decision`, `## Rationale`, `## Alternatives Considered`. The `/beads:decision` slash command from the upstream beads plugin (NOT vp-beads) wraps `bd create --type=decision` with structured templates and supports record/list/show/supersede operations. Decision lifecycle: open = active decision, closed = superseded or reversed (NOT closed-on-create).

## Acceptance Criteria

- retrospective skill recognizes when a sprint outcome is a decision (vs a task or feature)
- For decisions, prompts user and either invokes `/beads:decision` slash command OR creates via `bd create --type=decision` with the four-section template (## Decision, ## Rationale, ## Alternatives Considered, ## Affects)
- Decision-typed issues stay open while the decision is in force; retrospective doesn't auto-close them
- Cross-references the upstream `/beads:decision` slash command for the full lifecycle (record/list/show/supersede)
- Blocked by vocabulary story: depends on adopt-vocabulary story being completed first so decision type is documented

title: feat(synergy-tracker): stale-row detection via inverse-file glob in workflow 2 (Review)
description:
When this project retires a feature that the sibling tracks as 'aligned' (e.g., vp-knowledge retired PreCompact in v0.28.0; before today's reciprocation pass, our SYNERGY-vp-knowledge.md still tagged PreCompact aligned 2026-04-05), the sibling's row goes stale with no automated detection.

Concrete change: workflow 2 (Review) should optionally glob the sibling's SYNERGY file (if accessible at ../<sibling>/SYNERGY-<this-project>.md, or via the registry path-resolution from issue #4) and surface entries marked aligned that have measurable drift on this side, or features tracked as shared that no longer exist here. workflow 3 (Compare) already reads sibling files — workflow 2 can reuse the same access pattern.

Pairs with the reciprocation mandate (other issue, P2). Reciprocation puts the burden on the user; this issue automates the verification at scale.

Source: ../vp-claude/UPSTREAM-vp-beads.md (entry 3, 2026-05-04).
acceptance_criteria: Workflow 2 (Review) reads the inverse SYNERGY file when accessible; surfaces stale aligned rows and missing-this-side rows; degrades gracefully when sibling not accessible; check-hooks/remark pass.

title: docs(upstream-tracker): clarify redirect rule for sibling projects with shipped skill artifacts
description:
skills/upstream-tracker/SKILL.md line 83-84 currently reads: 'If the observation is about a sibling project rather than an upstream package or tool, redirect to /synergy-tracker — this skill only tracks upstream dependency friction, not cross-project patterns.' This conflates two distinct cases.

When a sibling project also ships upstream artifacts (the vp-plugins marketplace pattern: vp-beads, vp-knowledge, vp-git all consume each other's skills/hooks/agents), filing concrete bug reports, feature requests, or contribution opportunities against those artifacts IS upstream-tracker work — there is a real upstream artifact to file against. synergy-tracker is for patterns and divergences.

Concrete change: rewrite the redirect rule to distinguish patterns from filable artifacts —

  'If the observation is a cross-project pattern (shared approach,
  divergence, extraction candidate, capability gap) between sibling
  projects, redirect to /synergy-tracker. Sibling projects that ship
  upstream artifacts (skills, hooks, agents) can still receive
  upstream-tracker entries for concrete bug reports, feature requests,
  or contribution opportunities against those artifacts.'

Source: ../vp-claude/UPSTREAM-vp-beads.md (entry 7, 2026-05-04). vp-claude's UPSTREAM-vp-beads.md exists despite the existing rule because the carve-out is exercised in practice but undocumented.
acceptance_criteria: Redirect rule in skills/upstream-tracker/SKILL.md clearly separates pattern-class observations (→ synergy-tracker) from artifact-class filable items (still upstream-tracker); remark passes; skill-reviewer agent finds no regressions.

title: fix(validate-plugin): add agent and http to valid hook types
description: validate-plugin.mjs line 163 only accepts prompt and command as valid hook types. Claude Code also supports agent and http. Expand the check to include all four valid types.
close_reason: validate-plugin.mjs now uses VALID_HOOK_TYPES Set with 'command' and 'prompt'; agent and http are valid Claude Code types but kept out of our Set since we don't use them — the error message is now Set-based and extensible

title: docs(CLAUDE.md): expand Relationship to vp-knowledge section
description: Current section is 6 lines. Expand to document full integration surface: research-feeds-tracking, capture-synthesis mental model, shared BM ownership boundaries, marketplace co-installation.
close_reason: Relationship section expanded in v0.10.1 — now documents layered plugin architecture, hook delegation principle, and specific integration points table.

title: docs(CLAUDE.md): add hook conventions subsection
description: Document naming patterns, command-over-prompt preference, timeout conventions, matcher syntax, and CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT usage. Should land after PostToolUseFailure conversion so conventions reflect the all-command-hooks state.
close_reason: CLAUDE.md now documents hook type constraint (all hooks command type), paths field convention, parallel check validation, and W-shorthand fix — hook conventions are documented as part of v0.10.0

title: feat(sprint-review): add Boundaries section + output example + model:sonnet
description: Sprint-review agent has no Boundaries section (trigger restriction only in frontmatter) and no output-format example (all 4 examples show invocation only). The model:inherit choice is intentional — accepted as a deliberate difference from vp-knowledge's model:sonnet approach (see SYNERGY-vp-knowledge.md).
acceptance_criteria:
- agents/sprint-review.md gains a Boundaries section (mirrors the structure used in vp-knowledge agents) explicitly listing what sprint-review does NOT do (no file writes, no skill invocation, defers to /retrospective and /upstream-tracker)
- A 5th example added showing actual end-of-sprint output format (not just trigger invocation) — readers should be able to predict what the agent will produce
- model:inherit retained (deliberately different from vp-knowledge's model:sonnet — recorded in SYNERGY-vp-knowledge.md Divergences section as 'accept-difference')
- Trigger description in frontmatter still routes correctly on 'bd close', 'sprint done', 'what did we accomplish'
- npm run check passes; plugin-validator finds no regressions

title: feat(upstream-tracker): add argument-hint to frontmatter
description: Upstream-tracker is the only multi-workflow skill missing argument-hint. Add argument-hint: [workflow] [package-name] for consistency with synergy-tracker, backlog-groomer, vendor-sync.
acceptance_criteria:
- skills/upstream-tracker/SKILL.md frontmatter includes 'argument-hint: [workflow] [package-name]' (or equivalent shape that matches the skill's actual workflow + target arguments)
- Format consistent with the existing argument-hint values in synergy-tracker, backlog-groomer, vendor-sync
- npm run check passes (validate-plugin.mjs validates argument-hint field shape)

title: Log upstream beads CLI issues: bd init gitignore + bd memory search
description: Create UPSTREAM-beads.md with two Feature Request entries: (1) bd init should auto-gitignore interactions.jsonl and .beads-credential-key, (2) bd memory search for mid-session targeted context lookup. Both are upstream ownership with full/partial workarounds.
close_reason: Created UPSTREAM-beads.md with two Feature Request entries

title: Integrate SYNERGY-*.md across sprint-review, retrospective, backlog-groomer, and hooks
description: Add SYNERGY-*.md glob to sprint-review step 4, retrospective steps 2/3/7, backlog-groomer W1 step 5. Add SYNERGY dormancy nudge to session-start.sh, item 6 to precompact.sh, synergy-tracker mention to hooks.json PostToolUseFailure. Update CLAUDE.md layout/skills/conventions/cycle diagram, bump plugin.json to v0.9.0, add CHANGELOG entry.
close_reason: All cross-component integration complete: sprint-review, retrospective, backlog-groomer, hooks, CLAUDE.md, plugin.json, CHANGELOG

title: Extract upstream-tracker references/ directory for progressive disclosure
description:
upstream-tracker is ~3100 words and growing. Extract W6 target section template, generalization transform rules, and routing table to skills/upstream-tracker/references/basic-memory-friction-format.md. Matches vp-claude's progressive disclosure pattern where references/ hold templates while SKILL.md stays focused on workflows.

## Acceptance Criteria

- New file skills/upstream-tracker/references/basic-memory-friction-format.md exists
- SKILL.md W6 section references the file instead of inlining templates
- SKILL.md word count drops below 3000
- npm run check passes
- No functional change — same instructions, just reorganized
close_reason: Extracted routing table, target section template, generalization rules, and edit_note gotchas to references/basic-memory-friction-format.md. SKILL.md dropped from 3118 to 2923 words.

title: Add skill-reviewer to pre-release checklist
description: The plugin-dev:skill-reviewer agent caught 3 bugs in v0.5.0 that passed npm run check and manual review. Document running it as a standard pre-release step in CLAUDE.md releasing section.
close_reason: skill-reviewer now documented in release checklist (commit 260c8cd)

title: Enrich UPSTREAM entry format with severity, ownership, workaround
description: Research shows ownership ambiguity is #1 reason upstream bugs go unfixed. Add optional structured fields: severity (blocking/annoying/cosmetic), ownership (upstream/us/shared), workaround status (none/partial/full). Keep backward-compatible with existing entries.
close_reason: Added Severity/Ownership/Workaround structured fields to entry format

title: Explore new Claude Code hook events for sprint workflows
description:
New hooks available: PostCompact (verify UPSTREAM context survived), TaskCompleted (trigger upstream review), SubagentStart/Stop (lifecycle tracking). Evaluate which add value to vp-beads sprint workflow cycle.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Research each new hook event (PostCompact, TaskCompleted, SubagentStart, SubagentStop) for availability, matcher syntax, and supported hook types
- [ ] For each hook, write a one-paragraph evaluation: what it would do in vp-beads, estimated value, and implementation complexity
- [ ] Recommend which hooks to implement (if any) with priority and dependency ordering
- [ ] Create follow-up beads issues for any recommended hooks
notes: As of 2026-04-05, vp-knowledge v0.21.0 uses the `agent` hook type for real. The hook type is now validated in vp-beads v0.10.1 validate-plugin.mjs.

title: Spike: bd promote/wisps for capture-then-curate workflow
description:
## Goal

Evaluate whether vp-beads workflows (especially backlog-groomer or retrospective) could use `bd promote` and the wisps two-tier persistence model to support an "ephemeral capture, deliberate promotion" pattern.

## Findings

(fill in when complete)

## Background

Wisps are ephemeral pre-issues stored in the `wisps` table marked `dolt_ignored` — not Dolt-versioned, not synced across machines. `bd promote <wisp-id>` copies to the permanent `issues` table, preserving the original ID so all links survive, plus labels/deps/events/comments. A promotion comment records the reason. Two-tier persistence model: ephemeral capture (free, local-only) + curated promotion (synced).

## Acceptance Criteria

- Identify which vp-beads workflows could benefit (candidates: backlog-groomer's investigate-topic, retrospective's brainstorm-then-file, swarm-wave's exploratory waves)
- Workflow proposal OR "not worth it" rationale

title: Spike: bd gate for inter-wave coordination in swarm-wave
description:
## Goal

Determine whether vp-beads's swarm-wave skill could use `bd gate` (especially `gh:run`/`gh:pr` and bead-typed gates) for cleaner inter-wave coordination — and whether the resulting complexity is justified.

## Findings

(fill in when complete)

## Background

Beads v1.0+ has five gate types across four delivery phases: human (Phase 1), timer (Phase 2), gh:run / gh:pr (Phase 3, shells out to `gh`), bead (Phase 4, cross-rig with `<rig>:<bead-id>` await IDs). `bd gate add-waiter` registers agents for wake notifications when gates close. `bd gate discover` auto-matches GitHub workflow runs by branch+SHA+time-proximity. Resolved/escalated semantics distinguish success from failure.

Could replace ad-hoc shell-based blocking patterns in swarm-wave's post-wave gate workflow.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Use cases for gates in swarm-wave enumerated (post-wave checks, CI integration, cross-rig handoff)
- Proof-of-concept proposal OR rejection rationale
- Blocked by swarm-spike: if swarm-wave is being absorbed into bd swarm, this spike becomes moot

title: Explore observation normalization for UPSTREAM entries
description:
Normalize entry format inconsistencies across UPSTREAM files: missing Ownership fields, inconsistent severity vocabulary, entries without structured metadata. Inspired by vp-claude's knowledge-maintainer two-tier tag mapping (deterministic auto-fixes for unambiguous cases, confirmation for ambiguous). Future exploration.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Research document or design note describing the normalization rules
- Prototype either as a new workflow (W8) in upstream-tracker or as a hook
- No auto-normalization without user confirmation for content-level changes

title: Add PostToolUse Edit|Write hook for shell auto-formatting
description:
Auto-format hooks/*.sh with shfmt -w on every edit. Defensive since npm run check already runs shfmt -d, but prevents format drift between edits. Pattern from vp-claude's hooks/post-file-edit.sh.

## Acceptance Criteria

- PostToolUse hook in hooks.json matching Edit|Write for hooks/*.sh files
- Shell script runs shfmt -w on edited file, skips silently if shfmt not installed
- npm run check passes
- Hook timeout 5s
close_reason: Added PostToolUse Edit|Write command hook that auto-formats hooks/*.sh with shfmt -w. Skips silently if shfmt not installed. Pattern from vp-claude.

title: add schemas/ directory for RETRO and UPSTREAM structures
description:
Version-control the canonical schemas for RETRO-NN.md and UPSTREAM-*.md as machine-readable definitions (JSON Schema or similar). Enables PostToolUse validation and gives /retrospective and /upstream-tracker a spec to reference. Depends on PostToolUse hook design (vp-beads-3z0).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `schemas/` directory in plugin root with machine-readable definitions for RETRO and UPSTREAM formats
- [ ] Schema format chosen (JSON Schema, or markdown-with-frontmatter matching Basic Memory's pattern)
- [ ] PostToolUse hook (vp-beads-3z0) can reference the schemas for validation
- [ ] Schemas cover the v0.5.0 enriched UPSTREAM fields (Severity, Ownership, Workaround) as optional
- [ ] `npm run check` passes

title: add PostToolUse hook for UPSTREAM-*.md write validation
description:
After any Write/Edit to an UPSTREAM-*.md file, validate that the result conforms to the canonical structure (correct sections, entry format, no stale placeholders mixed with real entries). Depends on: (1) reference files defining the canonical format (vp-beads-emm), (2) deciding whether to use a prompt hook or command hook (prompt hooks can't access MCP tools — lesson from v0.5.0 PreCompact fix).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] PostToolUse hook in `hooks/hooks.json` with matcher for `Write` and `Edit` tool calls targeting `UPSTREAM-*.md` files
- [ ] Hook validates: correct section headings, no mixed placeholders with real entries, entry format matches the documented template (including optional structured fields)
- [ ] Hook reports validation errors as `systemMessage` without blocking the write
- [ ] Hook type chosen based on whether MCP tools are needed (command + additionalContext if yes, prompt if evaluation-only)
- [ ] `npm run check` passes

title: add reference files for UPSTREAM and vendor-registry formats
description:
Add skills/*/references/ directories documenting the canonical file formats: UPSTREAM-*.md structure (sections, entry formats, severity tags, upstream URLs), vendor-registry.json schema, and RETRO-*.md template. These give Claude a spec to validate against and help users understand the formats without reading SKILL.md in full. Needs content design before implementation.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Design the reference file format (markdown with fenced code blocks, JSON Schema, or both)
- [ ] Create `skills/upstream-tracker/references/upstream-format.md` documenting UPSTREAM-*.md structure including the v0.5.0 enriched fields (Severity, Ownership, Workaround)
- [ ] Create `skills/upstream-tracker/references/vendor-registry-format.md` documenting `.claude/vendor-registry.json` schema
- [ ] Create `skills/retrospective/references/retro-format.md` documenting RETRO-NN.md template
- [ ] Reference files are discoverable from the parent SKILL.md (linked or mentioned)
- [ ] `npm run check` passes

value: STALE — all items from the 2026-04-05 session were executed. The 2026-04-05 continuation session then performed 260+ edits: self-referentiality cleanup (13 notes), factual corrections (30+), observation recategorization (60+), Wessman career enrichment from CV + 4 research agents, 5 concept notes created (Stewardship Succession, Graceful Obsolescence, The Canary Pattern, Task-Boundary Collapse, Provenance Collapse), Flattr full history note, Note Quality Checklist. See BM session memory for details.

Workflow references: the codebase uses 'workflow N (name)' convention, not 'W3' shorthand. 41 numbered refs exist across 9 files. Many already include the name parenthetically but some don't. When writing new cross-references, always include the workflow name.
